mission.title: Install and Configure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS)
mission.description: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) is a managed Kubernetes offering that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on IBM Cloud. It is ideal for users looking for a robust, scalable solution without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on IBM Cloud for resource management and potential costs associated with cloud services.

steps.title: Install the IBM Cloud CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the IBM Cloud CLI, which is required to interact with IBM Cloud services.

```bash

curl -sL https://ibm.biz/idt-installer | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the IBM Cloud CLI.

steps.title: Login to IBM Cloud
steps.description:
Authenticate your IBM Cloud account to manage resources.

```bash

ibmcloud login

```

> Follow the prompts to enter your IBM Cloud credentials.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-create --name my-cluster --zone us-south-1 --kube-version 1.30 --num-workers 2

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local environment to access the new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are running as expected.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up additional features like autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --enable-autoscale

ibmcloud ks cluster-monitoring --cluster my-cluster --enable

```

> Enables autoscaling for your cluster.
> Enables monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-delete --cluster my-cluster --force

```

> Use --force to skip confirmation.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any remaining resources that may incur costs.

```bash

ibmcloud resource service-instance-delete my-cluster --force

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your actual resource name.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, upgrade to the latest version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-backup --cluster my-cluster

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup your cluster before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the specified Kubernetes version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running kubectl commands
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This typically indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources' when creating a cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the selected zone does not have enough available resources.

**Fix:**
Try selecting a different zone or reducing the number of worker nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the nodes are not properly initialized or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the node conditions with 'kubectl describe nodes' and resolve any reported issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found' when trying to access the cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster name may be incorrect or the cluster has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster name with 'ibmcloud ks clusters' and ensure it exists.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30 features including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.29, ensure that all workloads are compatible with 1.30 features.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' with all nodes in 'Ready' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the IBM Cloud CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
